title: Governed Decision Infrastructure
description: growth capital pitch deck cover — Sentra, 2026
speakerNotes:
Open with the thesis in one line: Sentra builds the governance layer that makes AI decisions accountable.

Beat: pause for two seconds. Let the cover land.

Then: 'For the next twelve minutes I'm going to show you a category, a product, and a market — and a real platform you can click on today.'

title: Problem: AI Without Accountability
description: Every AI recommendation happens without a source, approval gate, or outcome record
speakerNotes:
Three statistics to anchor the room:
  • 89% of enterprise AI deployments have no auditable decision trail.
  • Only 11% of regulated industries can answer 'why did the model recommend this?' under audit.
  • Average time to investigate one AI-driven incident: 14 days.

Name the pain in plain English: 'Today AI gives an answer. Nobody can prove where it came from, who approved it, or whether it worked.'

title: Category: Governed Decision Infrastructure
description: $16.3B market growing at 24.7% CAGR to $50.1B by 2030
speakerNotes:
Frame the category. We are not 'an AI tool.' We are the layer underneath every AI tool that an enterprise will eventually be required to install.

Analog: just as observability emerged underneath microservices, governance emerges underneath agentic AI.

Market: $16.3B today, $50.1B by 2030 — 24.7% CAGR. Source: composite of Gartner GRC + IDC AI Governance + analyst extrapolation.

title: Product: The Nine-Step Governance Loop
description: Signal, Context, Recommendation, Simulation, Policy, Execution, Proof, Outcome, Learning
speakerNotes:
Walk the loop end-to-end in under 90 seconds. Don't read every step — group them:
  1. Sense (Signal, Context)
  2. Decide (Recommendation, Simulation, Policy)
  3. Act (Execution)
  4. Account (Proof, Outcome, Learning)

Key line: 'Every step is hashed, versioned, and replayable. This is the audit trail regulators will demand by 2027.'

title: Demo: The Platform Is Real
speakerNotes:
TRANSITION TO LIVE DEMO HERE. Open Decision Theater in a second tab.

If time is short: skip to slide 6. The demo is in the appendix link.

If you do show it: pick ONE vertical (Vessels port-call decision is fastest — 90 seconds end-to-end). Show signal → recommendation → policy block → human approval → proof entry. Do not narrate every field; let the loop speak.

title: Part Two — Why This Wins
description: Divider into the moat: competitive differentiation, architecture, build evidence
speakerNotes:
Hold the divider for a full beat. This is the pivot from 'what we built' to 'why nobody else can catch us.'

One line: 'Part Two — why this wins. Not the demo. The moat.'

Then click straight into the market and competitive matrix.

title: Market: $16.3B to $50.1B
description: Competitive matrix — five capabilities no named competitor closes
speakerNotes:
Competitive matrix is the meat. Five capabilities across the top: provenance, policy-as-code, simulation, multi-domain, replayability.

DataRobot, Credo, Fiddler, Holistic AI — none ship more than two. We ship all five today.

If asked about hyperscalers: AWS Bedrock Guardrails and Azure AI Content Safety are filters, not loops. They block bad outputs; they don't account for decisions.

title: Domain Breadth: Six Verticals, One Loop
description: Sentra, Vessels, Terra, Carlota Jo, Command, Counsel — all governed by the same nine-step loop
speakerNotes:
The defensibility is the loop, not the vertical. We've already shipped six verticals on the same primitives:
  • Sentra — cyber resilience
  • Vessels — maritime risk and trading
  • Terra — real estate intelligence
  • Carlota Jo — private advisory
  • Command — unified ops
  • Counsel — legal matter intelligence

Message: 'Adding a seventh vertical is a configuration job, not a rebuild.'

title: Alloy — The Enterprise AI Hub
description: Introducing Alloy: unified front door to models, datasets, governance, and team controls
speakerNotes:
Transition: 'We just showed you six verticals on one loop. Now meet the surface that unifies all of them.'

Alloy is the flagship enterprise AI hub — a single entry point where models, datasets, spaces, governance records, and team controls converge. Four pillars: Foundry (model & data layer), Governance (proof + policy + audit), Fleet (every SZL surface), Enterprise (SSO, regions, RBAC, inference).

Key line: 'Alloy is not another dashboard. It's the operating system for governed AI.'

title: Alloy Foundry — The Model & Data Layer
description: HF Hub API integration: models, datasets, spaces, and governed inference playground
speakerNotes:
Foundry delegates to the Hugging Face Hub API — same contract, same endpoints, but every invocation passes through the nine-step governance loop.

Four tabs: Models (search + task filter), Datasets (live data preview), Spaces (embedded viewer), Inference (model selector + latency tracking).

Key line: 'Every model call is hashed and auditable. This is what regulated enterprises need before they can deploy AI at scale.'

title: Part Three — The Verdict
description: Divider into the close: commercial path, the ask, what we offer
speakerNotes:
Hold the divider. This is the room's signal that the pitch is closing.

One line: 'Part Three — the verdict. Commercial path, the ask, and what we offer.'

Then go straight into business model.

title: Business Model: Platform + Domain Pack
speakerNotes:
Pricing: platform license + per-domain pack. Enterprise band $50K–$500K ARR, anchored to decision volume and number of domains.

Three-phase commercial motion:
  1. Pilot (60 days, fixed-fee, one domain)
  2. Production (annual platform + one pack)
  3. Expand (additional packs, audit/compliance modules)

Gross margin target: 78%. Logo retention target: 95%.

title: The Ask
description: growth capital — use of funds, team plan, and the opportunity
speakerNotes:
The ask in one breath: 'We are raising a growth capital to harden the platform, ship two more verticals, and land the first ten enterprise logos.'

Use of funds: 55% engineering, 25% go-to-market, 15% security and compliance, 5% operating runway.

Close with the line of the deck: 'Every AI decision will eventually need an audit trail. We're already shipping the one that survives.'

Then stop talking. Let them ask the next question.
